title: Comcast to spin off its NBCUniversal and Sky media divisions
summary: The telecommunications giant will separate its media and technology businesses into two listed companies.

title: Hostilities between US and Iran cease and markets react positively
summary: Oil prices rise and Wall Street celebrates the truce, while experts warn about the persistence of the energy crisis.

title: Wall Street closes higher driven by the technology sector
summary: US indices rise thanks to the rebound in tech stocks and the inclusion of Alphabet in the Dow Jones.

title: Bitcoin falls below $60,000 and crypto ETFs record outflows
summary: Cryptocurrencies continue their downtrend as interest in spot ETFs wanes.

title: European stock markets trade mixed in a session without major changes
summary: The DAX remains stable while the Ibex oscillates between slight losses and gains near its highs.

title: Alphabet enters the Dow Jones Industrial Average
summary: The US index replaces a tech stock to gain exposure to the sector.

title: Yen hits lowest level against dollar in 40 years
summary: The Japanese currency weakens to 161.97 yen per dollar pressured by interest rate expectations.

title: Supreme Court rejects firing of Lisa Cook from the Federal Reserve
summary: The court decision prevents President Trump from dismissing the Fed governor, ensuring her tenure.

title: Prime ministerial candidate Burnham promises the greatest decentralization in history
summary: Promises to transfer powers to Manchester boost the pound and British bonds.

title: Rocket Lab acquires Iridium Communications and creates a vertically integrated space giant
summary: The acquisition consolidates the company's position in the space sector.

title: SpaceX launches mega bond issuance and attracts investor interest
summary: The space company seeks financing through debt as analysts debate whether it's time to buy.

title: OpenAI and DeepSeek expand their investments and workforce in artificial intelligence
summary: AI companies accelerate their growth with new chips, memories, and hiring to dominate the sector.

title: Consumer confidence and corporate credit improve in the eurozone
summary: June data shows a recovery in economic sentiment and an increase in lending to businesses.

title: US and British telecoms suffer from competition and debt
summary: Verizon and AT&T fall on the stock market while Virgin Media O2 sees its bonds plummet due to pressure from rivals.

title: Europe faces winter with low gas reserves and high prices
summary: Chevron's CFO points out that gas prices will remain high given limited supply.

title: Magnificent Seven stocks show mixed signals
summary: Amazon leads the day's gains but the group's total market cap has fallen more than $5 trillion from its highs.

title: Semiconductor sector faces declines and massive spending
summary: Samsung and SK Hynix fall amid $1.3 trillion investment plans, while one stock outperforms Micron.

title: IPO activity slows in Japan but accelerates in Spain
summary: Japan records the lowest number of stock market debuts in 15 years, while HIP, Digi and Ignis speed up to raise more than 1.5 billion.

title: SEC fines Bank of America's Merrill Lynch $7.5 million
summary: The regulator sanctions the bank for not properly reporting suspicious activities.

title: Mexico stock exchange suspends operations due to technical issue
summary: The computer glitch causes temporary interruption of the Mexican stock market.
